sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Celta Vigo have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, displaying attacking intent with an average of over 1 goal per game but struggling defensively without a clean sheet in 10 matches. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, have been in poor form, securing only 2 wins in their last 5 games and sitting at the bottom of the La Liga standings with just 5 points, indicating a lack of consistency and defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Celta Vigo have had the upper hand, winning both matches (2-0 at home and 1-0 away) against Real Sociedad. Historically, matches between these two sides tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 meetings, suggesting a tight contest could be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Celta Vigo rank decently in possession (6th) and final third entries (4th), indicating their ability to control games and create chances, though their conversion rate remains low (14th). Real Sociedad struggle offensively, with only 7 goals scored this season, and their defensive frailties (12 goals conceded) could be exploited by Celta’s attacking players like Iago Aspas and Borja Iglesias.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Celta Vigo, Iago Aspas (average Sofascore rating of 7.525) and Borja Iglesias (4 goals in last 5 matches) are in form and could pose a significant threat. Real Sociedad will rely on Álvaro Odriozola (rating of 8.0 in recent matches) to provide stability and creativity from the back, though their lack of consistent goal-scorers remains a concern.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo’s home advantage, recent head-to-head dominance, and slightly better form, they are favored to win with a 43% probability per Sofascore’s model. Real Sociedad’s poor league position (20th) and struggles on the road make a positive result unlikely, though a draw (34% probability) cannot be ruled out in a potentially low-scoring affair.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of this matchup between Celta Vigo and Real Sociedad ended with a narrow 1-0 lead for the home side at Estadio Abanca Balaídos. Celta Vigo managed to capitalize on their opportunity with a well-taken goal from Pablo Durán in the 20th minute. Despite Real Sociedad creating more big chances, they failed to convert, missing key opportunities. The game was marred by disciplinary issues, with Celta Vigo receiving a red card, leaving them a man down for the remainder of the match. This numerical disadvantage could play a significant role as the game progresses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo showed dominance in ball possession during the first half, controlling the game for large periods. Their ability to enter the final third frequently and win a high percentage of duels, especially in the air, helped them maintain pressure. However, their attacking output was limited after the goal, and the red card to Carl Starfelt has left them vulnerable defensively. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, looked more threatening in the final third with more entries into the penalty area and big chances created. Their inability to finish, however, has left them trailing at the break despite a stronger expected goals figure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw a back-and-forth battle in terms of attacking momentum. Celta Vigo started brightly with consistent pressure in the opening minutes, building up to a significant attacking spell around the time of their goal. They maintained a steady threat for a prolonged period post-goal before Real Sociedad shifted gears. The away side responded with notable attacking waves, particularly towards the end of the half, showing increasing intent to level the scoreline. Their momentum grew stronger in the closing stages, indicating they could carry this energy into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and discipline
halftimeSections.text: Pablo Durán stood out for Celta Vigo with his clinical finishing to secure the lead, while Óscar Mingueza's contribution in creating a big chance was vital. However, the red card to Carl Starfelt, coupled with an additional yellow, has put Celta in a precarious position defensively. For Real Sociedad, players like Sergio Gómez and Brais Méndez showed creativity in the final third, but finishing remains a concern with players like Mikel Oyarzabal and Yangel Herrera missing big opportunities. Real Sociedad will look to exploit their numerical advantage in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: With Celta Vigo down to ten men, Real Sociedad are likely to push hard for an equalizer early in the second half. Their ability to create big chances in the first half suggests they have the tools to break through a potentially fatigued and defensively weakened Celta side. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, will likely adopt a more defensive approach, looking to protect their lead with counter-attacking opportunities. The match could see heightened tension, and discipline will be crucial for both teams to avoid further setbacks.
